raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 3,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "R1.7": 2,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "Flags at least one claim the evidence does not support (e.g., 'ZeroLabel' pipeline has since become a standard reference for VLM-as-annotator work and is widely cited as evidence that VLM-derived labels can substitute for human annotations in downstream supervised pretraining').",
    "R1.4": "Names one prior work and articulates the delta (Radford et al., 2022, introduces 'ZeroLabel' pipeline; our work builds on this).",
    "R1.7": "Flags one missing or wrong citation (Veselovsky et al., 2023 not verified).",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (quantitative claims and citations flagged for verification).",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) (all requested dimensions addressed).",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (quantitative claims and citations flagged for verification).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (citation keys provided, but some not verified)."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 0.85
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: Flags at least one claim the evidence does not support (e.g., 'ZeroLabel' pipeline has since become a standard reference for VLM-as-annotator work and is widely cited as evidence that VLM-derived labels can substitute for human annotations in downstream supervised pretraining').
rationale.R1.4: Names one prior work and articulates the delta (Radford et al., 2022, introduces 'ZeroLabel' pipeline; our work builds on this).
rationale.R1.7: Flags one missing or wrong citation (Veselovsky et al., 2023 not verified).
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (quantitative claims and citations flagged for verification).
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) (all requested dimensions addressed).
rationale.RX.4: Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (quantitative claims and citations flagged for verification).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (citation keys provided, but some not verified).
